center.name: 阿德莱德
center.summary: 阿德莱德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括快速鸚鵡、Spotted scrubwren、Noisy miner，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Its range in South Australia has been steadily expanding since it was first recorded near Adelaide in the early 1890s. It is sedentary over its entire range. The noisy miner is territorial, and the territory of a colony is aggressively defended—which has led to a significant reduction in avian diversity in areas occupied by the noisy miner, with smaller species excluded. thumb|right|Open eucalypt forests without understory shrubs suit the noisy miner. thumb|Noisy miner (Manorina melanocephala), Melbourne, Australia The noisy miner primarily inhabits dry, open eucalypt forest without understory shrubs

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The spotted scrubwren (Sericornis maculatus) is a bird species native to coastal southern Australia, from Adelaide westwards to Shark Bay in Western Australia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species breeds in Tasmania during the summer and migrates north to southeastern mainland Australia from Griffith-Warialda in New South Wales and west to Adelaide in the winter
